title: Mutual Funds
description:
Mutual Funds are one of the most regulated and transparent investment products in India. A mutual fund pools money from thousands of investors and invests it in a diversified portfolio of stocks, bonds, or other securities managed by professional fund managers. All mutual funds in India must be registered with SEBI and managed by SEBI-registered Asset Management Companies (AMCs).

India currently has over 40 SEBI registered AMCs managing thousands of mutual fund schemes with a total corpus of approximately Rs 80 lakh crore. Mutual funds are subject to strict disclosure, governance, and investor protection regulations. Despite this, fraudsters create fake mutual fund schemes mimicking legitimate ones to collect investor money.

Key principle every investor must remember: ALL mutual fund investments are subject to market risk. No mutual fund — not even the most conservative debt fund — can legally guarantee returns. Any scheme promising guaranteed returns is not a mutual fund.
key_facts: All AMCs must be registered with SEBI under SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations 1996
India has 40+ SEBI registered AMCs managing Rs 80 lakh crore corpus
No mutual fund can legally guarantee returns
All mutual fund investments must go through KYC verified accounts
NAV (Net Asset Value) is published daily for all schemes
AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India) maintains registry at amfiindia.com
All mutual fund distributors must be AMFI registered (ARN holders)
Investors can verify schemes at amfiindia.com or sebi.gov.in
red_flags: Mutual fund promises guaranteed or assured returns of any kind
AMC name not found on AMFI website
Agent asks you to invest through unofficial apps or websites
No SEBI registration number provided for the AMC
Scheme not found on official AMC website
Agent pressures immediate investment without time to review
Returns promised far exceed category benchmarks
No KYC requirement — legitimate MF always requires KYC
Agent cannot provide AMFI Registration Number (ARN)
Investment link looks similar to but different from official AMC website
Agent asks for cash investment — all MF investments must be through banking channels
Scheme name very similar to a well known legitimate fund
how_to_verify: Go to amfiindia.com — official AMFI website
Click on Know Your MF → Fund Houses to verify AMC registration
Search scheme name on AMFI website to verify it exists
Verify AMC at SEBI website: sebi.gov.in → Registers → Mutual Funds
Check agent's ARN number on AMFI website
Invest only through official AMC website or SEBI registered apps like Zerodha, Groww, Paytm Money
Verify NAV daily on official AMC website or AMFI website
Call AMC customer care number listed on official website to verify scheme
real_case: SEBI has taken action against entities creating fake mutual fund schemes with names very similar to legitimate AMCs. Investors received professional-looking documents and were asked to invest in schemes that did not exist. Track B verification against SEBI and AMFI databases would have instantly flagged these schemes.

quiz.question: A mutual fund agent guarantees 25% annual returns on their equity fund. What should you do?
options: Invest — a good fund manager can guarantee returns
Invest half your savings to be safe
Refuse — no mutual fund can legally guarantee returns
Ask for the guarantee in writing
quiz.explanation: No mutual fund can legally guarantee returns. Any guarantee is a fraud signal — report this agent to SEBI.

quiz.question: Where do you verify if a mutual fund AMC is registered?
options: Google search
The agent's visiting card
AMFI website (amfiindia.com) and SEBI registry
Newspaper advertisements
quiz.explanation: Always verify on AMFI's official website and SEBI's registry. These are the only reliable sources.

quiz.question: What is the full form of AMFI?
options: Association of Mutual Funds in India
Authority for Mutual Fund Investments
Agency for Market Fund Integrity
Association of Market Finance Institutions
quiz.explanation: AMFI stands for Association of Mutual Funds in India. Their website amfiindia.com is the official registry.

quiz.question: An agent asks you to invest in a mutual fund by paying cash. What should you do?
options: Pay cash for convenience
Pay cash but ask for a receipt
Refuse — all MF investments must be through banking channels
Pay half cash and half cheque
quiz.explanation: All mutual fund investments must be through banking channels. Cash investments are not permitted and indicate fraud.

quiz.question: What is KYC in the context of mutual funds?
options: Know Your Category — choosing the right fund type
Know Your Credentials — agent verification
Know Your Customer — investor identity verification mandatory for all MF investments
Know Your Capital — minimum investment amount
quiz.explanation: KYC (Know Your Customer) is mandatory identity verification for all mutual fund investors. Any scheme not requiring KYC is fraudulent.

quiz.question: What does NAV stand for in mutual funds?
options: Net Annual Value
Net Asset Value — the per unit price of a mutual fund scheme
Nominal Asset Valuation
National Asset Value
quiz.explanation: NAV (Net Asset Value) is the per unit price of a mutual fund scheme, published daily. You can verify it on the AMC website.

quiz.question: A scheme called 'HDFC Guaranteed Growth Fund' approaches you for investment. HDFC AMC is a legitimate registered AMC. What should you check first?
options: Invest immediately since HDFC is a trusted name
Verify if this specific scheme exists on HDFC AMC official website and AMFI
Invest a small amount to verify legitimacy
Check Google reviews for this scheme
quiz.explanation: Fraudsters use names of legitimate AMCs for fake schemes. Always verify the specific scheme on the official AMC website and AMFI.

quiz.question: What is an ARN number and why is it important?
options: Account Registration Number — your investor account number
AMFI Registration Number — mandatory for all legitimate mutual fund distributors
Annual Returns Number — projected returns figure
Asset Risk Number — risk rating of the scheme
quiz.explanation: ARN (AMFI Registration Number) is mandatory for all legitimate mutual fund distributors. Always ask for and verify the agent's ARN.

quiz.question: Under which regulation are all mutual funds in India governed?
options: SEBI (Portfolio Managers) Regulations 2020
SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations 1996
SEBI (CIS) Regulations 1999
SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations 2013
quiz.explanation: All mutual funds are governed under SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations 1996.

quiz.question: If you suspect a mutual fund scheme is fraudulent, where should you report it?
options: Police station only
SEBI SCORES at scores.sebi.gov.in or call 1800 266 7575
Consumer court only
Reserve Bank of India
quiz.explanation: Report mutual fund fraud on SEBI SCORES at scores.sebi.gov.in or call SEBI helpline 1800 266 7575.
